operating_state: Total revenues declined to $200.9 billion from $246.3 billion driven by lower commodity prices and reduced volumes across all segments. Net income attributable to Chevron fell to $21.4 billion from $35.5 billion as upstream earnings dropped significantly while downstream margins compressed. Income from equity affiliates decreased to $5.1 billion from $8.6 billion due to reduced contributions from major joint ventures.
cost_structure: Purchased crude oil and products costs decreased tracking lower commodity prices while operating expenses remained stable. Depreciation increased to $17.3 billion from $16.3 billion including upstream asset impairments primarily affecting California operations due to regulatory challenges. Properties, plant and equipment additions totaled $26.7 billion driven by the PDC acquisition and ongoing capital investments.
material_events: Completed PDC Energy acquisition for $6.5 billion in August 2023 adding Denver-Julesburg Basin assets and issuing 41 million Chevron shares. Announced definitive agreement to acquire Hess Corporation for $53 billion in all-stock transaction expected to close mid-2024 subject to regulatory approvals. Returned $26.4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Recorded charge for abandonment obligations from previously sold Gulf of Mexico assets due to purchaser bankruptcies.
macro_exposures: Faces climate change litigation from multiple U.S. jurisdictions seeking damages related to fossil fuel production and Louisiana coastal erosion cases. Ecuador judgment of $9.5 billion remains under international arbitration with favorable tribunal rulings to date. Exposed to commodity price volatility with derivative losses and environmental liabilities concentrated in downstream operations.
forward_outlook: Hess acquisition expected mid-2024 pending FTC clearance after December 2023 Second Request with management anticipating transaction synergies. Capital expenditures increased indicating sustained investment in growth projects while maintaining committed credit facilities. Holds suspended exploratory well costs with development decisions expected within five years for majority of projects.
uncertainty_profile: Hess merger faces regulatory approval risks including potential antitrust conditions or delays with integration execution risks around synergy realization. Cannot estimate liability ranges for climate litigation due to unprecedented nature though management believes claims lack merit. Unrecognized tax benefits with ongoing examinations while foreign tax credit carryforwards expire through 2033.
